steps.title: Set off the opening phrase
steps.content: The phrase "A conceptual artist and designer embraced by both the art world and the fashion world" describes Mary Ping. It is an introductory noun phrase before the main clause.

steps.title: Use only a comma
steps.content: Choice A leaves no punctuation between the modifier and the main clause. Choice B uses a colon after a fragment. Choice C uses a semicolon after a fragment. A comma correctly separates the introductory description from "Mary Ping was chosen." The answer is D.
